program.name: NAB Graduate Program 2027
program.rotation_text: You will complete rotations during the program. You can also do a four-week secondment in a business area of your choice.

eligibility.work_rights: You must be an Australian citizen, a New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time of applying. Temporary visa holders are not eligible.
eligibility.graduation_window: You must have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in the past three years (2024, 2025 or 2026), or be due to complete it by the end of January 2027.
minimum_requirements: Be an Australian citizen, a New Zealand citizen, or an Australian permanent resident at the time of applying.
Not be a temporary visa holder.
Have completed an undergraduate or postgraduate degree in 2024, 2025 or 2026, or be due to complete it by the end of January 2027.

stages.name: Online application
stages.details: You complete an online application form where you share your academic and work experience, and nominate your chosen business area.

stages.name: Online assessment
stages.details: If you meet the eligibility criteria, you may be invited to complete online assessment. The online assessment includes a personality assessment, verbal reasoning, numerical reasoning, and logical reasoning.

stages.name: Video interview
stages.details: If you are successful in the online assessment, you progress to video interviews where you record responses to questions on a secure site.

stages.name: Engagement centre
stages.details: The final stage is an engagement centre with interactive activities. Engagement centres are usually held in May/June and may be via Zoom or in person depending on the business area.

stages.name: Offer
stages.details: If you are successful, NAB will call you a few days after your engagement centre with the outcome.

tips: Choose your business area carefully because you cannot change it after submitting your application.
Upload your most recent academic transcript. If you completed postgraduate studies, upload both your undergraduate and postgraduate results.
For online assessments, make sure you are free from distractions and have a good internet connection. Read questions carefully and try practice questions beforehand.
Prepare for the video interview by thinking about school, work, and life examples you can use, and how they relate to the role.
Prepare for the engagement centre by doing mock interviews and practising behavioural-based interview answers.
Relocation to participate in the graduate program is self-funded.
If you need reasonable adjustments for accessibility, you can email NAB to discuss support.

exec_themes: Helping customers transition to a net zero economy (including funding and advice for decarbonisation)
Growing sustainable/environmental finance (e.g., green and sustainability-linked lending and bonds)
Focusing sustainability priorities on climate action, housing affordability, and First Nations economic advancement
Building internal climate capability and embedding climate considerations into everyday banking and credit decisions
Improving NAB’s own operational environmental performance (renewable electricity and emissions reduction targets)
commercial_context.profit_engine: NAB’s sustainability pages describe a relationship-led banking model that supports customers through finance and advice (including sustainable finance products like green/social/sustainability bonds and sustainability-linked loans). These activities indicate NAB aims to earn income through lending, capital markets activity, and related banking services provided to customers, including as they invest in decarbonisation and resilience.
commercial_context.headwinds: Climate change is described as creating “significant risks and opportunities” for the economy and for NAB. NAB also flags that fully embedding climate considerations “throughout the bank” remains a challenge, and notes growing expectations for climate-related disclosures and reporting (including preparation for Australian Sustainability Reporting Standards – AASB S2 Climate-related Disclosures from FY26).
commercial_context.esg: NAB states its sustainability approach focuses on making a positive long-term impact for customers, communities, and the environment. It prioritises three customer support areas: climate action, housing affordability, and First Nations economic advancement. On climate, NAB’s stated goal is net zero by 2050 across financed and facilitated emissions and operations, supported by an environmental finance ambition of $80bn (1 Oct 2023 to 30 Sep 2030), renewable electricity (100% in 2025), and an operational emissions reduction target (72% reduction in market-based Scope 1 and 2 by 2030 vs a 2022 baseline). NAB also describes a risk-based approach to ESG risk, including responsible lending/investment policies and climate-risk disclosure aligned to TCFD in FY25, and preparation for AASB S2 in FY26.
commercial_context.recent_pivot: NAB says its climate strategy was refreshed in 2025, shifting emphasis toward leveraging its “relationship-led” strengths to support customers to reduce emissions and finance related opportunities. NAB also highlights a “new environmental finance ambition” of $80bn for the period 1 Oct 2023 to 30 Sep 2030.

notes: Program dates (applications open/close and start date) were taken from the NAB Graduate careers page key dates and cross-checked against the 2027 job ad.
Streams and locations were taken from the 2027 job ad.
Program length (15 months) was stated in the 2027 job ad.
Rotations and the four-week secondment were stated on the NAB Graduate careers page and repeated in the 2027 job ad.
Salary was not stated on the sources used.

notes: The NAB site pages used are official but are mostly sustainability disclosures and product descriptions; they do not provide a full, investor-style explanation of the end-to-end profit model (e.g., net interest margin, fee mix), so the profit_engine summary is inferred at a high level from the described lending and capital markets activities.

The 2025 Annual Report and 2025 Climate Report are linked from NAB’s sustainability hub, but the PDFs could not be fetched by the tool (fetch returned an error), so this extraction relies on the accessible official web pages rather than the full reports/transcripts.
